questions.question: In the following question, four words are given out of which one word is incorrectly spelt. Find the incorrectly spelt word.
questions.explanation: The correct spelling is Indigenous.

questions.question: In the following questions, four words are given, out of which only one word is correctly spelt. Find the correctly spelt word.
questions.explanation: The correct spellings of the other words are: penchant, archetype and stereotypical. D is the right answer.

questions.question: In the below question, a sentence has been given in Direct/Indirect speech. Out of the four alternatives suggested, select the one which best expresses the same sentence in Indirect/Direct speech. "I am not offering it for Miss Smith's collection," said he.
options: He said that he does not offer it for Miss Smith's collection.
He said that he had not offered it for Miss Smith's collection.
He said that he did not offer it for Miss Smith's collection.
He said that he was not offering it for Miss Smith's collection.
questions.explanation: When converting direct speech to indirect speech, the present continuous tense is changed to past continuous tense. Option D is the right answer. Option A is eliminated, as it is in the simple present tense. Option B is eliminated as it turns the original tense into past perfect tense. Option C is eliminated as it changes the original tense into simple past tense.

questions.question: In the below question, sentence/passage is split into four parts and named P, Q, R & S. These four parts are not given in their proper order. Read the sentence/passage and find out which of the four combinations is correct. P. Could Cartwright possibly find a messenger to send round with those papers? Q. He lifted the receiver, dialed the number, and asked for Cartwright's extension. R. He had begun to write, when he remembered that he had not yet picked up the papers he had left with Cartwright. S. He would be very grateful.
questions.explanation: Sentences P and S are a pair: P describes a favor asked by the speaker, while S is used to express the speaker's gratitude should the favour be granted. Option D is eliminated. Following the chronological order of events that took place, Sentence R should begin the passage, followed by Sentence Q. Options B and C are eliminated. Option A is the right answer.

questions.question: In the below question, sentence/passage is split into four parts and named P, Q, R & S. These four parts are not given in their proper order. Read the sentence/passage and find out which of the four combinations is correct. P. I'm glad that we don't depend on local eating. Q. I'm grateful for all those exported oranges and lemons, bunches of lettuce, packs of spinach, heads of broccoli. R. Where we live on the Canadian border, local eating traditionally meant that by springtime, people, deprived of vitamin C, were suffering from "winter sickness," more commonly known as scurvy. S. There's a lot we can't do on our own.
questions.explanation: Sentences P and R are a pair: P expresses an opinion of the author, while R gives us the author's reason for holding that opinion. Options A and D are eliminated. Sentences Q and S are also a pair: Q tells us about the exported fruits and vegetables that the author expresses gratitude for, and S follows by concluding the passage, and telling us that it is not possible for a country to do everything on its own, implying thereby that it must depend on others for exporting certain food items. Since sentence S is a conclusion of the passage, it is clear that the pair PR must begin the passage. Option C is eliminated. Option B is the right answer.

questions.question: According to the passage, the French are most likely to:
questions.explanation: The last sentence of the passage mentions that Americans are likely to downplay their 'dejectedness' which means they may act happier than they actually are whereas, the French are likely to under-report their happiness which means they might be happier than they show. Options B and C are essentially the same and are ruled out. The correct choice is option A.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the phrase '...the living disproof Donald Trump...', in the context of the passage?
options: A satirical remark on Donald Trump.
A maxim based on Donald Trump's character.
A dilemma expressed by the author.
A laudatory statement for Donald Trump.
questions.explanation: The sentence states that more a person runs after power, money etc. their sense of self-worth is likely to be lower and Donald Trump is an exception to this rule. The sentence thereby describes Trump as someone who runs after power and money and yet his sense of self-worth hasn't diminished. The phrase is thus, a satirical (mockery, sarcasm) comment. Maxim- a short, pithy statement expressing a general truth or rule of conduct Dilemma- difficulty, problem Laudatory- expressing praise and commendation

questions.question: According to the passage, which of the following is likely to heighten the risk of heart disease?
questions.explanation: It is clearly mentioned in the passage that 'work', over which people do not have much control heightens the risk of heart diseases in people.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true, according to the passage?
options: Nations with higher inequality are more likely to breed mental illness.
Competitive ethos can trigger mental illness in winners as well as losers.
About a third of Britons admit to feeling depressed at times.
questions.explanation: All statements except the third one are true. A third of American adults and about half of those in Britain admit to feeling depressed sometimes.

questions.question: According to the passage, America's upbeat culture:
options: Contributes to inequality in the country.
Is conducive to its citizens' well-being.
Contributes to dejectedness felt by Americans.
questions.explanation: America's upbeat culture is described as 'pathological', hence, there is a negative connotation to it. Hence, options A and B can be immediately ruled out. Further, the author says that the upbeat culture of Americans tend to downplay their dejectedness.

questions.question: Out of the fours alternatives, choose the one which can be substituted for the given words. One who is a hater of reasoning
questions.explanation: misogynist - a man who hates women misanthropist - someone who dislikes other people and avoids involvement with society bibliophile - a person who loves books

questions.question: Out of the four alternatives, choose the one which can be substituted for the given words/sentences. That which cannot be expressed in words
questions.explanation: Inveigh: To speak or write about with hostility. Illegible: Incapable of being read Incorrigible: Not able to be changed or reformed

questions.question: Some parts of the sentences have errors and some are correct. Find out which part of a sentence has an error choose the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, choose "No Error" option. The Prime Minister said that (A)/ everyone must keep there (B)/ surroundings clean. (C)/ No error (D)
questions.explanation: The correct sentence is 'The Prime Minister said that everyone must keep their surroundings clean'. 'There' expresses existence or location. 'Their' expresses possession. Here 'their surroundings' is the correct usage because it refers to one's surroundings.

questions.question: Some parts of the sentences have errors and some are correct. Find out which part of a sentence has an error choose the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, choose "No Error" option. He got affected by the disease (A)/ when he was (B)/ a teenager. (C)/ No error (D)
questions.explanation: The sentence is correct as it is.

questions.question: A sentence is given with a blank to be filled in with appropriate word. Four alternatives are suggested. Choose the correct option. When I was a teenager I too had shown _____ disregard for the advice of my parents
questions.explanation: Candour: honesty, truthfulness Callow: Immature Capricious: unpredictable Cogent: convincing The appropriate word to use here is callow meaning immature. A teenager is immature and because of that he disregards the advice of elders

questions.question: The sentence below has a phrase in brackets. From the options, choose the option that improves the bracketed part. I once knew a poor demented old man who (believed that him is a horse), and would stand by the hour together before a manger, nibbling hay.
options: believed himself to be a horse
believed to be a horse himself
believed to have been a horse
questions.explanation: Option B is eliminated as it does not make it clear as to whether it was the man who believed himself to be a horse, or someone else believed him to be so. Option C is eliminated, as it changes the meaning of the sentence, and conveys that others believed him to be a horse. Option D is eliminated as it is ungrammatical. Option A is the right answer, as it makes the sentence more succinct and clearly expressed. We need the reflexive pronoun "himself" to refer back to the subject "old man".

questions.question: The sentence below has a part in brackets. From the options, choose the option that improves the bracketed part. Under miserable conditions of life, (any vision of the possibility of betters things) makes the present misery more intolerable, and spurs those who suffer to the most energetic struggles to improve their lot.
options: any vision of the betterment of possible things
any vision of the possibility of making things better
any vision of the possible of improving upon things
questions.explanation: Option a changes the course of the highlighted part. Option c is incorrectly framed as we need the noun'possibility' in place of the adjective 'possible'. Comparing options b & d, "making things better" is more appropriate as using "betters things" is grammatically incoherent in the given context.

questions.question: Choose the word opposite in meaning to the given word. Ingenious
questions.explanation: Ingenious means having the skill and imagination to create new things. Hence the word opposite in meaning is "Unimaginative"

questions.question: Choose the word similar in meaning to the given word. Repudiate
questions.explanation: Repudiate means refuse to accept. Hence, 'reject' is similar in meaning.

questions.question: Choose the word similar in meaning to the given word. Promulgate
questions.explanation: Promulgate means to promote or make widely known. Hence, "Broadcast" is similar in meaning.

questions.question: Sentences given with blanks to be filled in with an appropriate word(s). Four alternatives are suggested for each question. Choose the correct alternative out of the four. Beside the numerous buildings, there ______ an old man who is known for his far sightedness
questions.explanation: We will use present tense as the latter part of the sentence signifies that the sentence mentions about a current situation (is known..). Hence, C and D are eliminated. Now the subject for the verb is "Man" and not "Buildings", and hence, we will use singular verb. So, "Lives" is correct.

questions.question: Out of the five alternatives, choose the one which is opposite in meaning to the given word. Remiss
questions.explanation: Remiss means lacking care or attention to duty; negligent. Lax means careless. Indolent means lazy. Relict means a widow. Therefore, opposite is Dutiful. Example: Remiss in his duties, the security guard slept all over the night without knowing about the burglary.

questions.question: Four alternatives are given for the idiom/phrase in bold. Choose the correct alternative that best expresses the meaning of the idiom/phrase. To pick holes
questions.explanation: To pick holes means to find faults in something. Here holes means faults or mistakes. Think of this as analogous to a customer who approaches a service station to fix a punctured tyre and shows one puncture after the other when each one is fixed.

questions.question: Four alternatives are given for the idiom/phrase in bold. Choose the correct alternative that best expresses the meaning of the idiom/phrase. By the skin of the teeth.
questions.explanation: By the skin of the teeth means very narrow. Our teeth has an outer covering of Enamel. It is very thin. So by the skin of the teeth means very narrow escape.

questions.question: A sentence has been given in Active/Passive Voice. Out of the four alternatives suggested, select the one which best expresses the same sentence in Passive/Active voice The teacher has punished him for telling lies.
options: He has been punished by the teacher for telling lies.
He was punished by the teacher for telling lies.
He was being punished by the teacher for telling lies.
He had been punished by the teacher for telling lies.
questions.explanation: Since the sentence is in present perfect tense (has punished), the same tense will be used with the participle form (has been punished). A is the right answer. B is in the simple past tense, C is past continuous and D in past perfect tense.
